title: Findings (round 1)
description: Codex output for round 1 of a v2.0 review cycle. Strict-mode compatible: additionalProperties:false on every object, every property in required.

confidence.description: 1=guess, 2=plausible, 3=likely, 4=high confidence, 5=verified (tool output, test ran, doc cited)

contradicts_grounding.description: v2.1 rail (spec §5.1): set true when this finding's category is one a deterministic tool covers (gofmt/golangci-lint for style, staticcheck/go vet for unused/deadcode, gosec for taint, govulncheck for vulns, race for visible races) AND that tool passed clean on the cited file:line AND you have no reproducible failure to cite. NEVER set true on categories the tools cannot judge: correctness, design, security (semantic), test_quality (semantic), maintainability (semantic). When true, the engine demotes the finding to display-only so it cannot block. When false (default), the finding stays at its claimed severity.

line_scope.description: v2.1 rail (spec §6): which payload section does this finding land in? `changed_line` = on a line in CHANGED; the author owns it and it can block. `change_triggered_context` = on a CONTEXT line that the change caused/surfaced; still the author's responsibility, still can block. `pre_existing_unrelated` = on a CONTEXT line the change did not touch or trigger; the engine routes these to display-only and they NEVER drive must-address regardless of severity. Test: if reverting just the CHANGED diff makes the issue go away, it's change_triggered_context; otherwise pre_existing_unrelated.

raised_by_angle.description: v2.1 PR 9 (spec §6 consensus rule): for Tier-1 cycles, round 1 runs in parallel through multiple perspective lenses (security/correctness/architecture). Each angle tags its findings via this field. The engine groups findings by file:line; consensus findings (raised by ≥2 angles) drive must-address, singletons are demoted to display-only. When this field is absent or equals 'default', the finding came from the single-reviewer path and is not subject to consensus filtering. Optional field — non-Tier-1 cycles emit it absent or 'default'.
